summary: Fixed the GitHub Pages publishing workflow by enabling the page generation job, adding top-level Pages permissions and concurrency, configuring Pages before upload, switching to the dedicated upload-pages-artifact action, and upgrading deploy-pages to v4. The PR body says it hopefully resolves issue #1085. No review discussion was present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added top-level GitHub Actions permissions for `contents: read`, `pages: write`, and `id-token: write`.
Added top-level Pages deployment concurrency group `pages` with `cancel-in-progress: false`.
Removed `if: false` from the `gen-pages` job, enabling page generation to run.
Changed page artifact upload from generic `actions/upload-artifact@v3` with artifact name `pages` to `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3` using the generated output path.
Removed the deploy job's intermediate artifact download and re-upload steps.
Moved permissions and concurrency out of the deploy job into workflow-level settings.
Upgraded Pages deployment action from `actions/deploy-pages@v1.2.4` to `actions/deploy-pages@v4`.
Kept deployment gated to the repository default branch with `if: github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch`.
smart_moves: Used GitHub's purpose-built Pages artifact flow: `configure-pages`, `upload-pages-artifact`, then `deploy-pages`, instead of a generic upload/download artifact bridge.
Removed the disabled-job guard (`if: false`) so the workflow can actually generate pages.
Kept deployment restricted to the default branch, preserving a safety gate for published documentation or status pages.
Upgraded Pages-related actions to current major versions, aligning the workflow with newer GitHub Pages deployment expectations.
Set required OIDC and Pages permissions explicitly, which is necessary for modern `deploy-pages` workflows.
decomp_lessons: Infrastructure fixes can unblock project visibility and automation even when they do not touch decompilation source code.
For GitHub Pages workflows, prefer the current dedicated Pages action sequence over generic artifact upload/download plumbing.
Keep Pages permissions explicit and near the workflow top level when both generation and deployment jobs participate in a Pages workflow.
Use branch guards for deployment jobs so generated artifacts can be produced in CI while publication only occurs from the default branch.
When modernizing Actions workflows, remove obsolete workaround steps instead of layering new Pages actions on top of old artifact transfer logic.
naming_conventions: Workflow jobs retain descriptive kebab-case IDs: `gen-pages` and `deploy-pages`.
Step names use clear imperative/descriptive phrases such as `Set up GitHub Pages`, `Upload GitHub Pages artifact`, and `Deploy to GitHub Pages`.
Environment variables use uppercase names such as `IMAGE_REPO`, `REGISTRY`, `OUTPUT`, and `IMAGE`.
follow_up_queries: Check issue #1085 for the exact failure mode this Pages workflow fix addressed.
Verify whether later PRs further updated `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml` or changed Pages deployment behavior.
Search for other workflows still using older `deploy-pages`, `upload-artifact`, or `download-artifact` patterns for Pages publication.
